info.title: SlideRule Earth API
info.description:
On-demand processing of NASA ICESat-2, GEDI, SWOT, and CASALS satellite data.

## For AI Agents

**Use only `/arrow/*` endpoints.** These return standard file formats (Parquet,
CSV, GeoParquet, Feather) that can be saved and analyzed with pandas/pyarrow.
Do NOT use `/source/*` streaming endpoints — they return binary serialized
records that require the SlideRule Python client to decode.

**Workflow:**
1. POST to `/arrow/{api}` with parameters and `"output": {"format": "parquet"}`
2. Save the binary response body to a file (e.g. `result.parquet`)
3. Query locally with DuckDB, pyarrow, or pandas:
   - `import duckdb; duckdb.sql("SELECT * FROM 'result.parquet' LIMIT 10")`
   - `import pandas as pd; df = pd.read_parquet("result.parquet")`

**Keep regions small.** Time ranges can be wide (multi-year is fine for
time series analysis), but spatial regions should be tight. If you get
HTTP 429 (rate limited) or 503 (cluster saturated), wait 30-60s and
retry with a smaller spatial region.

## Quick Start
1. Pick an API: atl03x, atl06x, atl08x, atl13x, atl24x, gedi04ax, gedi01bx, gedi02ax, casals1bx
2. Construct a polygon (counter-clockwise, closed ring, first == last point)
3. POST to `/arrow/{api}` with `{"parms": {..., "output": {"format": "parquet"}}}`
4. Save response to file and read with pyarrow/pandas

## Column Schemas
Use `GET /source/schema?api={name}` to discover column names, types, and
descriptions for any API at runtime. Or see the component schemas below.

## Discovery
- GET /source/openapi — complete OpenAPI 3.1 spec with live column schemas
- GET /source/schema — column schemas for all APIs
- GET /source/defaults — full parameter tree with defaults
- GET /source/version — server version
- GET /source/health — cluster health

RegionMask.description: Raster mask for complex coastlines (alternative to poly).

fields.description: Restrict output to these columns only

asset.description:
Registered raster name. Available assets:

**Elevation / DEMs:**
- arcticdem-mosaic — ArcticDEM 2m mosaic (PGC, Arctic coverage)
- arcticdem-strips — ArcticDEM 2m strips with temporal info (PGC, Arctic)
- rema-mosaic — REMA 2m mosaic (PGC, Antarctica)
- rema-strips — REMA 2m strips with temporal info (PGC, Antarctica)
- esa-copernicus-30meter — Copernicus DEM COP30 global 30m (ESA/OpenTopography)
- usgs3dep-1meter-dem — USGS 3DEP 1m DEM (US coverage)
- usgs3dep-10meter-dem — USGS 3DEP 10m DEM (US coverage)
- merit-s3 — MERIT DEM ~90m global elevation model
- gebco-s3 — GEBCO global bathymetry/elevation (~450m)

**Land Cover / Canopy:**
- esa-worldcover-10meter — ESA WorldCover 2021 global 10m land cover
- meta-globalcanopy-1meter — Meta Global Canopy Height 1m (ALS/GEDI-derived)
- landsat-hls — Harmonized Landsat/Sentinel-2 30m multispectral (requires catalog)

**Bathymetry:**
- bluetopo-bathy — BlueTopo bathymetry

**GEDI Gridded:**
- gedil3-elevation — GEDI L3 gridded elevation
- gedil3-canopy — GEDI L3 gridded canopy height
- gedil3-elevation-stddev — GEDI L3 elevation standard deviation
- gedil3-canopy-stddev — GEDI L3 canopy height standard deviation
- gedil3-counts — GEDI L3 shot counts
- gedil4b — GEDI L4B gridded biomass

**GEDTM:**
- gedtm-30meter — GEDTM 30m terrain model
- gedtm-std — GEDTM standard deviation
- gedtm-dfm — GEDTM difference from mean

**SWOT Simulated:**
- swot-sim-ecco-llc4320 — SWOT simulated SSH (ECCO LLC4320 model)
- swot-sim-glorys — SWOT simulated SSH (GLORYS model)

cnf.description:
Confidence filter. Default: ["atl03_low","atl03_medium","atl03_high"].
Integer shorthand: -2=TEP, -1=noise, 0=background, 1=within10m,
2=low, 3=medium, 4=high. Use -1 for all photons.

ParquetResponse.description: Apache Parquet file. Columns per API documented above.

RateLimited.description: HTTP 429 — throttled. Retry after delay or 30-60s.

ServerBusy.description: HTTP 503 — cluster is saturated. Retry after 30-60s or reduce region size.

StreamingResponse.description:
Binary stream of serialized RecordObjects. Each record has a header
(type string + length) followed by the payload. Use the SlideRule
Python client to deserialize. Stream ends with an empty terminator.

get.description:
Returns complete parameter tree by plugin: cre, icesat2, gedi, swot.
Use this to discover parameters not documented in this spec.

get.summary: Complete OpenAPI 3.1 spec with live column schemas
get.description:
Returns the full OpenAPI spec as JSON with column schemas injected
at runtime from the schema registry. AI agents and API tooling can
use this URL to discover all endpoints, parameters, and response schemas.

get.summary: Response column schema for an API
get.description:
Returns the Parquet column schema (names, types, descriptions) for a
given arrow API endpoint. Without the `api` parameter, returns the
list of available APIs.

Example: GET /source/schema?api=atl06x

parameters.name: api
parameters.description:
API name. Use `atl03x-phoreal` for the PhoREAL variant.
Omit to list all available APIs.

list_apis.summary: No api parameter — list available APIs

apis.atl03x-phoreal: PhoREAL vegetation extent metrics (replaces atl03x base columns)
apis.atl03x-surface: Surface-fitted elevation segments (replaces atl03x base columns)
apis.atl03x-blanket: Surface blanket elevation segments (replaces atl03x base columns)
apis.atl08x: Land and vegetation height segments (ICESat-2 ATL08)

post.description:
Most flexible API. Base columns: ATL03xBaseColumns.
Algorithm blocks change the output:
- phoreal → REPLACES base with ATL03xPhoREALColumns (extent-level vegetation)
- surface → REPLACES base with ATL03xSurfaceColumns (fitted elevation segments)
- blanket → REPLACES base with ATL03xBlanketColumns (top-of-surface segments)
- yapc → yapc_score always present in base columns; use yapc block to tune
- atl24 → atl24_class, atl24_confidence present in base columns (populated when enabled)
- samples → appends RasterSampleResponseColumns

with_dem_sampling.summary: Photons + co-located DEM and land cover

post.description:
Fitted surface segments for glaciers and ice sheets.
Tune with: res, len, cnt, ats, maxi, sigma_r_max, H_min_win.
Response: ATL06xResponseColumns (24 columns verified live v5.3.0).

Lake/reservoir water levels. Response: ATL13xResponseColumns.
Use atl13 config block to filter by water body name or refid.
Photon classification for shallow water. Response: ATL24xResponseColumns.

Above-ground biomass density. Response: GEDIL4AxResponseColumns.
GEDI-specific filters: degrade_filter, l2_quality_filter,
l4_quality_filter, surface_filter (see /source/defaults "gedi").

Streams ATL03 photon-level records for a single granule.
Returns binary serialized atl03rec records.
Use the Python client or /arrow/atl03x for Parquet output.

Geolocated waveforms with transmit/receive samples.

Elevation and canopy height metrics.

post.description:
Reads a single dataset from an HDF5 file with optional row/column slicing.
Returns binary serialized h5dataset records.

post.summary: Read multiple HDF5 datasets from one file (streaming)
post.description: Reads multiple datasets from a single HDF5 file with slice specifications.

post.summary: Read HDF5 groups as Parquet DataFrame
post.description:
Creates DataFrames from HDF5 groups and outputs as Parquet.
Supports geo-indexing and raster sampling.

Sample one or more raster datasets at specified lat/lon coordinates.
Returns JSON with sampled values.

coordinates.description: Array of [lon, lat] or [lon, lat, height] coordinate arrays

post.summary: Extract raster subsets by bounding box
post.description: Extract raster data for specified bounding boxes.

extents.description: Array of [xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax] bounding boxes

post.description: Coordinate conversions, polygon splitting, intersection, spatial index operations.

get.description: Converts between GPS, GMT, DATE, CDS time formats.

post.description: Streams events for the specified duration. Use type bitmask to filter.

tags.name: ICESat-2 Streaming
tags.description:
ICESat-2 streaming record APIs (s/p-series). Returns binary serialized
RecordObjects — requires the SlideRule Python client to decode.
AI agents should use the /arrow/* Parquet endpoints instead.

tags.name: GEDI Streaming
tags.description:
GEDI streaming record APIs. Returns binary serialized RecordObjects —
requires the SlideRule Python client. AI agents should use /arrow/* instead.
